subject: Anthropology
title: Acheulean Hand Axes
description: A quick, practical guide to Acheulean Hand Axes.

hook.text: A teardrop-shaped stone, perfectly symmetrical, sharp on both sides. Someone spent hours making it beautiful.

buildup.text: Acheulean hand axes appeared about 1.7 million years ago and were shaped on both sides — a major leap in skill.

discovery.text: They were used for butchering, digging, and woodworking. The same design persisted for over a million years.

twist.text: Their symmetry suggests aesthetic sense. These tools are more refined than function demands — crafted with pride.

climax.text: Acheulean tools show foresight, planning, and possibly teaching. Making one requires a mental template.

punchline.text: Beauty entered the world through stone.

quiz.question: What distinguishes Acheulean from Oldowan tools?
options: They are shaped on both sides with greater symmetry
